methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Redwood Materials is a Series D climate and energy company founded by J.B. Straubel that has raised $2.3B+ to date and carries a last post-money valuation of $6.0B+. The peer universe consists of 9 comparable companies—1 public (LNZA) and 8 private—including TAE Technologies, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, QuantumScape, and Form Energy at similar or adjacent stages.

Two valuation methods triangulate the company's value. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $6.0B Series D post-money, yielding $9.0B. Peer-median methodology examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M), producing a median of $1.0B and a range of $700M to $2.0B. The peer-median approach reflects substantial valuation dispersion within the cohort.

These two methods produce a triangulated range of $850M (low) to $10.3B (high), with a median of $5.0B. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-progression assumptions and peer selection volatility. The current $6.0B+ valuation sits above the peer median but materially below the stage-progression case, suggesting either a valuation premium relative to near-peer companies or limited comparability within the peer set. Key uncertainties include technology-to-revenue trajectory risk and downstream capital intensity requirements.
